effort_complexity.rationale: Small diff over shared plumbing; the codex envelope unknowns are the substance (the original S63 deferral reason).

design_decisions.choice: codex CLI non-zero-exit ErrKind mapping: spec.json AC-03 is internally inconsistent ("same ErrKind mapping as the claude driver" vs. a stated value of `provider`, while claude's own ratified mapping is `auth`). Proposed default: map codex's non-zero exit to ErrKindAuth, matching claude's ratified mapping, to preserve internal/run/slice.go:487's terminal-halt-on-auth fail-fast for both subprocess drivers.
options: ErrKindAuth — matches claude driver's ratified mapping (S02 pin 2); preserves terminal-halt-on-auth fail-fast parity across both subprocess drivers (implementer's proposed default)

ErrKindProvider — matches spec.json AC-03's literal parenthetical value; treats codex's non-zero exit as a distinct provider-side failure class, but silently loses terminal-halt-on-auth fail-fast for a codex-CLI auth/login failure

New codex-specific ErrKind (not yet defined) distinguishing an auth-looking non-zero exit from a genuinely different provider failure, via stderr pattern-matching — rejected by S02 precedent for the same reason S02's pin 2 rejected it (missed pattern on CLI wording drift silently reintroduces the gap)
design_decisions.rationale: See design.md decision 6 for the full analysis. Not resolved by the implementer per Rule 9 — flagged for Captain design review before this Kind value is locked into TestCodexErrorMapping.
